judge.reason: The study directly examines the association between aspirin use and reduced risk of colorectal cancer in humans with diabetes, providing hazard ratios and confidence intervals for the effect of aspirin on colorectal cancer incidence.

papers.title: Low-dose aspirin use after diagnosis of colorectal cancer does not increase survival: a case-control analysis of a population-based cohort.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The study examines survival outcomes, not incidence or risk reduction of colorectal cancer, which is the focus of the question.

papers.title: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs and colorectal cancer risk in a large, prospective cohort.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, N.I.H., Intramural; Research Support, U.S. Gov't, Non-P.H.S.

judge.reason: The study directly examines the association between aspirin and non-aspirin NSAID use with reduced colorectal cancer risk in a large cohort of humans, providing human outcome evidence for the question.

papers.title: Aspirin for Colorectal Cancer Prevention: Age Matters.

judge.reason: The paper discusses concerns about aspirin use for colorectal cancer prevention in the elderly but does not provide direct evidence on aspirin's effect on colorectal cancer risk.

papers.title: Can Cost-effectiveness Analysis Inform Genotype-Guided Aspirin Use for Primary Colorectal Cancer Prevention?

judge.reason: The paper uses a decision analytical model simulating a cohort of adults to evaluate the cost-effectiveness and clinical outcomes of genotype-guided aspirin use for colorectal cancer prevention, providing evidence on aspirin's impact on colorectal cancer risk.

papers.title: Aspirin use and survival after diagnosis of colorectal cancer.

judge.reason: The study directly examines the association between aspirin use after colorectal cancer diagnosis and reduced colorectal cancer-specific and overall mortality, providing human outcome evidence for the question.

papers.title: Activation of the AMP-activated protein kinase by the anti-diabetic drug metformin in vivo. Role of mitochondrial reactive nitrogen species.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't; Research Support, U.S. Gov't, P.H.S.; Retracted Publication

judge.reason: The paper directly investigates the mechanism of metformin-induced AMPK activation in vivo, showing that mitochondrial reactive nitrogen species (RNS) are involved, which directly addresses the question about the mechanism of metformin AMPK activation.

papers.title: Genetic downregulation of AMPK-alpha isoforms uncovers the mechanism by which metformin decreases FA uptake and oxidation in skeletal muscle cells.
papers.journal: American journal of physiology. Cell physiology
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The paper directly investigates the mechanism of metformin-induced AMPK activation in skeletal muscle cells, showing differential activation of AMPK isoforms, but the evidence is cellular rather than clinical.

papers.title: Convergence of IPMK and LKB1-AMPK signaling pathways on metformin action.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The paper investigates the role of IPMK in metformin-induced AMPK activation in IPMK(-/-) murine embryonic fibroblast cells, providing mechanistic evidence for the mechanism of metformin AMPK activation.

papers.title: Metformin regulates glucose transporter 4 (GLUT4) translocation through AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK)-mediated Cbl/CAP signaling in 3T3-L1 preadipocyte cells.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The paper provides cell-based evidence for the mechanism of metformin-induced AMPK activation and its downstream effects on GLUT4 translocation, directly linking metformin to AMPK-mediated signaling pathways.

papers.title: Role of AMP-activated protein kinase in mechanism of metformin action.

judge.reason: The paper directly demonstrates that metformin activates AMPK in hepatocytes, providing a mechanism for its metabolic effects.

papers.title: Metformin enhances the chemosensitivity of hepatocarcinoma cells to cisplatin through AMPK pathway.

judge.reason: The paper directly investigates the mechanism of metformin's effect on chemosensitivity through the AMPK pathway in hepatocarcinoma cells, providing cellular evidence for the mechanism of metformin-induced AMPK activation.

papers.title: Prognostic significance of AMPK activation and therapeutic effects of metformin in hepatocellular carcinoma.
papers.journal: Clinical cancer research : an official journal of the American Association for Cancer Research
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The paper directly investigates the mechanism of metformin-induced AMPK activation in hepatocellular carcinoma, showing its effects on cell growth and signaling pathways, but the context is specific to HCC rather than a general mechanism.

papers.title: Metformin directly inhibits ghrelin secretion through AMP-activated protein kinase in rat primary gastric cells.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The paper directly tests the mechanism of Metformin's effect on ghrelin secretion through AMPK in rat primary gastric cells, providing evidence for the mechanism of Metformin's action on AMPK activation.

papers.title: Metformin attenuates diabetic neuropathic pain via AMPK/NF-κB signaling pathway in dorsal root ganglion of diabetic rats.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The paper demonstrates that metformin activates AMPK in diabetic rats, providing mechanistic evidence for its effect on neuropathic pain, which is relevant to the question about the mechanism of metformin AMPK activation.

papers.title: Metformin activates KDM2A to reduce rRNA transcription and cell proliferation by dual regulation of AMPK activity and intracellular succinate level.
papers.pubtypes: Journal Article; Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't

judge.reason: The paper provides mechanistic evidence showing that metformin activates AMPK and reduces intracellular succinate levels, both of which are required for KDM2A activation, which is relevant to the question about the mechanism of metformin AMPK activation.

papers.title: Why MUC16 mutations lead to a better prognosis: A study based on The Cancer Genome Atlas gastric cancer cohort.

judge.reason: The study uses a human cohort (The Cancer Genome Atlas) to explore the mechanisms behind MUC16 mutations leading to better prognosis, indirectly addressing the role of p53 mutation in cancer prognosis.

papers.title: [Research advances of K-ras mutation in the prognosis and targeted therapy of gastric cancer].

papers.title: The Functional Role of Long Non-coding RNA UCA1 in Human Multiple Cancers: a Review Study.

judge.reason: The paper discusses the role of UCA1 in various cancers but does not directly address p53 mutation or its relationship to cancer prognosis.

papers.title: A Novel Mutation in MYH Gene Associated with Aggressive Colorectal Cancer in a Child: A Case Report and Review of Literature.

judge.reason: The paper describes a case of pediatric colorectal cancer with specific mutations (TP53, SMAD4) and discusses their potential impact on prognosis, though it is a single case report rather than a systematic review or trial.

papers.title: Estimating the predictive power of silent mutations on cancer classification and prognosis.

judge.reason: The paper evaluates the predictive power of silent mutations in cancer classification and prognosis using 9,915 cancer genomes, directly addressing the role of p53 mutations in cancer prognosis.

papers.title: Colorectal cancer prognosis: is it all mutation, mutation, mutation?

judge.reason: The paper examines genetic mutations in colorectal cancer and their association with survival outcomes, directly addressing the role of p53 mutations in prognosis, though it focuses on colorectal cancer rather than a broader cancer context.

papers.title: Gynecologic cancer as a "sentinel cancer" for women with hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer syndrome.

papers.title: Genomic mutation characteristics and prognosis of biliary tract cancer.

judge.reason: The study identifies prognostic factors (KRAS mutations, VEGFA pathway mutations, and higher TMB) in biliary tract cancer, which is relevant to the question about p53 mutation and cancer prognosis, though the focus is on a different cancer type and different prognostic markers.

papers.title: Prognostic implications of BAX and TGFBRII mutations in colon cancers with microsatellite instability.

judge.reason: The paper examines the prognostic implications of BAX and TGFBRII mutations in colon cancers with microsatellite instability, providing survival data for a human cohort, though the question is about p53 mutation and cancer prognosis.
